# services.gs1200-exporter

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.gs1200-exporter`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.address` | `string` | IP address or hostname of the GS1200 switch. |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.debug` | `boolean` | Enable debug logging. Logs are accessible via journalctl -u gs1200-exporter. |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable gs1200-exporter. |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.json` | `boolean` | Output logs in JSON format. Logs are accessible via journalctl -u gs1200-exporter. |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.passwordFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to a file containing the password to log in to the GS1200 web interface. This is the recommended option as it avoids storing the password in the Nix store. Compatible with sops-nix and agenix. |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port on which to expose Prometheus metrics. |
| `services.gs1200-exporter.verbose` | `boolean` | Enable verbose logging. Logs are accessible via journalctl -u gs1200-exporter. |
